channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: why does this keep happening — 27 replies — 108.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-05-23 07:30 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62263631
>>62263230
The question is why, why use BTC with 200x mcap to suppress LINK

62263646
>>62263631
SN=SN and he's not your friend.

62263816
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
link

62263870
>>62263230
Take your meds, its literally just following the btc chart. All the alt charts are the same.
Btc pumps = alts pump
Btc dumps = alts dump

Idk why you retards think link is the one causing btc to pump and dump when its the other way around

62264096
>>62263870
Sure thing rabbi

62264135
Coinbase constantly has positive notifications sent out for link, but the price is always shidding and farding.
For example: pic related

62264172
>>62263870
>ts literally just following the btc chart.
Bitcoin was crabbing, and the second Link pumped and hit $10, Bitcoin dumped.

Are you blind.

62264180
>>62264172
sweetie you forgot your medicine :}

62264354
>>62263230
because you're retarded enough to think that BTC is dumping because of LINK's movement rather than LINK is dumping because of BTC's movement.

62264389
>>62263473
why does this bitch have such a pointy schnoz, is she a kike?
t. link investoor (newfag)

62264394
>>62263870
>Btc pumps = alts pump
>Btc dumps = alts dump
More like
Btc pumps = alts dump
Btc dumps = alts dump
simple as

62264410
>>62264354
No he understands that BTC dumps, to drag link down, before it pumps too high

Just DCA anons, they'll unleash it when they're ready

62264427
>>62264410
Wrong you dumb link nigger. EVERYTHING dumps when BTC dumps. Every single coin has TA levels called resistances and supports. Shitcoins like LINK won't break its resistance without permission from BTC (permission being BTC breaking through its own resistance level) so LINK will just sit at resistance waiting on BTC's permission to go higher. If BTC dumps then LINK will dump without ever breaking the current resistance because it is a shitcoin and BTC controls movement.

Your inability to understand this is why you're poor and still bagholding absolute garbage after all these years. Hope this helps.

62264474
>>62264427
retard

62264483
>>62264474
stay poor bagholder

62264512
>>62264474
we'll never let u live it down stinker
you lost

62264795
>>62264172
Btc spiking is exactly what caused link to go to $10 dumbfuck

62264817
We will dump BTC
We will turn hanta into covid 2.0
We will pop the AI bubble
We will start WW3
We will do whatever it takes, and there's nothing you can do about it linkie

62264876
>>62263230
>Link pumps to $10
>pumps
Does anyone else remember when linkies could've exited for $30+?

62264877
>>62264427
>EVERYTHING dumps when BTC dumps.
That's not responding the argument being made m8. The claim is that link pump > BTC dump - not BTC dump > link dump. If you were contending with the thread seriously, the counterpoint should be that bitcoin dumps when ANYTHING pumps. But that wasn't your counterpoint. Because it doesn't happen. And you know you're being disingenuous.

62264884
>>62264877
you're a poor bagholding loser who has held for 8 years and missed countless opportunities.

62264934
>>62264877
you're a poor bagholding loser who has held for 8 years and missed countless opportunities.

62265147
>>62263870
Sure that's why shit like tron and hype pumped while BTC crashed..kys

62265193
>>62264795
>Btc spiking
what the fuck are you smoking

item.title: THE DECOUPLING HAS ARRIVED — 37 replies — 50.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
THE DECOUPLING HAS ARRIVED

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-05-23 06:54 UTC

OP

The mad lad really did it bros, check the charts

Early replies (first 3)

62261996
OH FUCK IM GONNA DECOUPLEEEEE

62262000
>>62261990
>>62261996

KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!!!!!!!!!!

SO FXCKING BULLISH CHAINLINK SAAAAAAAAAAAAR

62262002
Kabooooom

Links going to $20 by end of month!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62262046
>>62261990
Top is in, for crypto in general.

62262054
>already dropping
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62262062
>>62262013
YEH SERGEY! FUCK IM UP!

62262285
Do you guys think the run to 1k is going to be a scenario where you wake up and link is up 300% and everyone is losing their minds, or do you think it's going to be a 10 year run of +2% days and never having a red day again?

62262300
time to crash btc i guess

62262350
>what's that? link at $10!
>pull it.

62262393
>LINK hits 10.03
>BTC takes a dive

Im just not gonna sell

62262397
ok boomers

62262405
>>62262285
after all the fake and gay market manipulation bullshit, it deserves to go straight to 1k in a single candle

62262418
>>62262405
This is exactly how XRP holders think

62262427
>>62262418
>don't believe your lying eyes goy!
bitcoin is used to manipulate the price of link, I don't give a fuck about your whataboutisms

62262533
>>62262427
Its funny how by doing that shit they erased 99% of people from buying Link but the 1% that bought have metric fuckloads and a vengeance. Almost worse.

62263186
>>62262418
XRP isn't getting adopted by DTCC later this year.

62263224
>>62262285
Link is never going close to 40 ever again kek and the only way it makes a new ath, is BTC going upwards of 500k. Very unlikely scenario for the next 15 years

62263238
>>62263224
>the only way it makes a new ath, is BTC going upwards of 500k
Nah the only way Link makes a new ath is BTC not dumping every time Link moves.

62263256
>>62263238
Bro cmon, be honest, we're at literally at 0.00012 sats, we got raped by Sirgay and it's over. We have lost, it's just another shitcoin and it will never recover in any meaningful way, while king shitcoin has a chance of making new aths.

62263278
>>62263256
>we got raped by Sirgay
Fucking how?

We got raped by 6 years of Bitcoin dumping on every single Link pump and announcement.

62263287
>>62263278
Are you retarded?
I literally bagholded link from .00169 sats to the shit it is now at 0.00012, i would had made it having sold all my Link and converted to BTC, now I'm a bagholder and definitely selling all my unstaked shit if it reaches 20-25

62263293
>>62263287
Yes, because we got raped by 6 years of Bitcoin dumping on every single Link pump and announcement.

62263337
>>62263293
Whatever bro, we were at 20 bucks at what 2019-2020? It's been 6 years and we're not even at 10. BTC is at 77k and shitting itself still.
Link has failed for more and I'm tired of pretending it hasn't. Build rewards? Staking? All shit same as link, fuck that fat fucking scammer. Satoshi nakamoto my ass

62263373
Dude... the thing isn't moving at all

62263385
>>62263337
this but unironically

62264177
>>62263337
See >>62263293

62264242
>>62262285
spike to $1k for a whole minute then dump back to $10 never to rise again

62264451
>>62261990
We finally did it lads

Let the seething resume

62264756
>>62261990
The switch has been flipped!

62264777
>>62262030
>Well I sold 1k to realize some profits for the probable reckoning coming
This is a "its going to go lower so I may as well sell" post subconciously disguised as a "my investment did well and its time to reap the rewards"

And yes I'm a licsensed phsychiatrist. Take care of your mental health in Q3 bro.

62264863
>>62264777
Those digits. Not really, it was like $900 dollars and I cut my realized loss for the year from 10k to 9k. If I sold em all it’d be closer to 20k realized loss but I’m just gonna hang in there. Need to be more patient instead of exiting so early. Still annoyed about intel but whatever. The pain of selling it all and it ripping is infinitely larger than me holding through a drop to sub $5

62264991
>>62264756
switch flipped

62265135
>>62261990
Check the chart, not decoupled from BTC LOL

item.title: How do I progress my career as a junior lawyer? — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do I progress my career as a junior lawyer?

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-23 07:31 UTC

OP

How do I progress my career as a junior lawyer?

I am a lawyer working in a very niche commercial law area, and I am fearful that I have pigeonholed myself into a career that does not have upward mobility. How can I more effectively apply for jobs at top tier firms and transition my career?

I have all the credentials, I have billed over $700,000 in the last year, I excel at upselling clients, and I am very personable.

Despite these factors, I am stressing that my career is dead in the water. The firm I work at pays almost minimum wage, and I want to improve my situation. All my applications are met with auto-rejections, offers of low pay, being told I am over-qualified, or being told that I am too junior in my career to make the jump to greener grass.

Background:

I have a Liberal Arts undergrad (double majored in legal studies and journalism), a Law degree, and relevant post-graduate studies.

I interned for three years at a mid-tier commercial firm that effectively no longer exists, completed three short contract stints as a paralegal (2 - 6 months at three different firms. Multinational NGO, boutique commercial firm, and a criminal firm). I learned a lot at each of these, getting courtroom experience, flying into and out of other sates for work, and learning the basics. I was made redundant at two when they could not afford to retain any juniors, and the third did not have the financial capabilities to extend my contract.

I now also have a little over a year at my current firm. I enjoy the work, however, I am fearful that given how niche it is I might not be growing my career. It is commercial law but a very niche aspect of it.

What should I do?

Early replies (first 3)

62265003
if you are asking /biz/ about your legal career, yes, it is dead

62265013
>>62265003
Sick burn, bro

62265038
>>62264989
I am not a lawyer I know nothing about law firms

but I am a business owner and I do know that the sort of person I'd take on as a partner is either a competitor or an employee capable of starting a competing business.

I would humbly suggest that the instant you either strike out on your own or start asking questions about the business side of things you might meet with greater success. Law I believe is one of those professions where working for other people doesn't pay that great, but starting your own firm mints millionaires fast.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265050
>>62265038
I do not think that I am yet ready to start my own business, that might be a goal which I will achieve in the next five to ten years, but currently I am still at a stage where there are issues which I cannot solve without a more senior lawyer to bounce ideas off of.

I really do love the idea of starting my own business someday though. The sales aspect is absolutely one of my favourite parts of my job.

62265055
>>62265050
keep learning, you'll get it.

another thing to keep in mind is starting your own business doesn't necessarily mean losing your mentors. Over the years I've let a few employees go so they could strike out on their own. I still give them advice if they need it. A competitor isn't necessarily an enemy, and also you don't necessarily have to work in direct competition with your current firm. Though that depends exactly HOW niche we're talking. But often moving to a different area to start a biz will avoid competition and keep your former employer around as a resource or even to partner on big jobs

62265121
>>62264989
I scored a 178 on the LSAT while stoned when I was 19. What did you score?

62265191
>>62265055
This is might doxx me, but I'll risk it. I work in Trust Law / pension planning. Basically just cook up trust documents, and our country's version of a pension fund, and deal with that.

It is very niche in that, the big firms offer it, however, they overcharge and underdeliver, and the few firms that offer competitive rates (and grab the market share) are firms with economy of scale. Meaning, they might only be a team of five to ten lawyers, but they do the vast majority of the work in the country. Outside of my firm, which is, arguably, one of the best, the competitors are few and far between. They are so niche that my boss knows most of their leaders, haha.

>I still give them advice if they need it.
That is incredibly altruistic of you. You should be very proud of that.

>moving to a different area to start a biz
I would love to do this someday. The main areas my skills are currently tailored to trust law, commercial work, and I am pretty decent at migration law. However, migration law has the difficulty of requiring a pipeline of migrants, and beating out the firms that already have that established can be tricky I imagine.

How did you decide which area to carve out your niche in?

Also, would you say that it is better to be a jack of all trades, master of none, or just a master of one?

62265195
>>62265121
Congratulations, that is very impressive. Did you go on and work in law?

item.title: A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn… — 12 replies — 28.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn…

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-05-23 07:12 UTC

OP

A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking. Does that mean if I’ve staked 2000 LINK, I’d earn $2000 per day?
Is that true, or was the anon just talking bullshit?

Early replies (first 3)

62258684
complete bullshit. if they opened staking to everyone and diverted 100% of all revenue to stakers(never happening), each link would make you less than a penny a day.

good thing holders are retarded and you can just make up bullshit for a decade tho

62258761
yes, but a loaf of bread will also cost 2000

62259325
test

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62259833
>>62258598
>sergei will deny again

62259895
>>62258598
2 in the pink, 1 in the LINK

62261509
>>62258598
Yes my friend your math is not a challenge. It is to be this for us and to every one of our communities so good and wealth will be extreme. We will to be in good spirits and also very full with lots of food and enjoyment for our families and lives! Go go gadget LINK!!!!

62262042
Please be honest with me, bros... no jokes, no meme answers, no shitposts, just 100% honesty. What price will Chainlink realistically go to?

62262203
>>62262042
3,5 - 4 $

62262214
>>62262042
It is to be happening with you and me both my brother very very soon with much haste and wealth for good lifes!!!! We will stand up like Iron man next to Thor and cry victory!

62264939
>>62262042
$8

62265165
>>62262042
llm models will say 80-100 in a couple years

62265171
>>62265165
llm models are not fortune tellers, stinkie

item.title: Stonk idea — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Stonk idea

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-23 07:35 UTC

OP

Will longing take two interactive be easy money if GTA 6 comes out this date? What I mean is fast % upwards after launch and cashing out with a set sell order. I mean even if it’s slop sell records will be hit with a big hypetrain and marketing campaign to add to it.

Early replies (first 3)

62262129
>>62262109
Trump is president so it won't be delayed. It was delayed because you don't want those dems in office when a game like this drops.

62262156
Goycattle can't time these things. I don't really know why they can't, but they can't. They just lack the finesse.

62262160
>>62262129
So sound short-term investment or you think the gains will be minimal/long-term more likely? I really don’t want to hold a gaming company for much longer after the release of a big game.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62262390
>>62262160
Depends on how popular the game gets. Also, if their are record sales numbers it could affect earnings which would drive the stock up too.

62262439
Buy the rumor, sell the news.

62262594
>>62262439
That pic is amazing lmao

62265204
>>62262390
I mean isn’t this the most anticipated game ever?

They fucked over their grandkids beyond belief and don't see any of it as their fault hahaha

item.title: This stock is literally rat poison quadrupled. — 4 replies — 7.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
This stock is literally rat poison quadrupled.

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-23 07:07 UTC

OP

This stock is literally rat poison quadrupled.

No reality based in anything, just complete emotion.

-7% for a week while it smashed earnings, meanwhile everything else is shooting up like crazy 4-5% gains EVERY SINGLE DAY.

AMD 1M is up 54%
Nvidia 1M is up 7%. Literally poison.

Early replies (first 3)

62264735
>>62264699
What do you expect out of a stock that's up 1300% over 5 years and already holds the #1 spot by marketcap

62264762
>>62264699
I sold Nvidia when it hit $220 a few weeks ago. The past few quarters earnings report made me realize it's the most heavily manipulated stock right now. Only institutional investors decide when it dances

62265151
>>62264735
>holds the #1 spot by marketcap
this isn't a real reason tho
>"well, good work everyone, but we have #1 marketcap, time to stop growing!"
it's a spook, a fake constraint that sounds to people who don't understand the core ideas behind the economy (ie. the point of all of this)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265162
>>62264735
The problem with being #1 is that there's only 1 direction left to go.

item.title: Investing in Chinese stocks - Good Idea? Your experience? — 6 replies — 6.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Investing in Chinese stocks - Good Idea? Your experience?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-23 06:43 UTC

OP

Investing In Chinese Stocks — Key Takeaways
>Major Risks & Political/Regulatory Issues
Government Intervention Risk: The government can make entire sectors vanish overnight. "Look at what happened to private education stocks."
Rule-By-Party / Arbitrary Actions: China doesn’t have rule of law — holdings "could effectively be wiped out on whim."
Executives And Crackdowns: CEOs and firms can be targeted for political reasons. "On top of my head, in China, company's CEO can disappear after saying the wrong thing. Remember Jack Ma?"
Past "Rug Pulls" And Sudden Regulation: "Anyone who experienced the government rug pull a few years back would understand the risks. A whole sector went -90% overnight..."

>Access, Structure, And Practicalities
A-Shares vs B-Shares vs HK/ADR Routes: Buying mainland A-shares has residency/qualification limits; foreigners often use Hong Kong listings or ADRs. "If you want to invest in Chinese stocks, the most common ways are investing in ADRs (US markets), or in HK markets."
Settlement / Market Mechanics: Mainland markets differ (e.g., T+1 settlement, A-share dominance). "Settlement is T+1, therefore no intraday trading possible. Most stocks are of the A-share class."
Local Platforms Exist For Residents: Chinese banking apps and services (Alipay, bank platforms, ExpatInvest) let residents buy funds/stocks. "my chinese banking app (cmb) has investment options built in... Alipay, you can buy fund and debt"

Early replies (first 3)

62265087
>>62265085
>Community Views On Returns & Strategy
Mixed Long-Term Performance: Some say long-term returns exist but are volatile; others warn China trails the S&P and is opaque. "Chinese stocks over the very long term haven’t been very bad... but of course below the S&P."
Prefer HK/Filtered Exposure Or ETFs: Many recommend Hong Kong listings or quality-filtered China ETFs rather than direct A-share exposure. "I trade on HK stockmarket... I think it is better to trade HK stocks rather than A stocks"
Use ETFs / International Diversification: Some suggest broad international or EM ETFs instead of concentrated China bets. "Just get an all inclusive emerging markets index etf"

>Common Practical Warnings & Advice
Be Careful With Liquidity & Exit Situations: Consider what happens with travel/visa problems or sudden regulation; selling may be hard. "There's also liquidity concerns too... what will happen to your investments if some covid like event happens, will you even be allowed to sell..."
Many Redditors Advise Caution / Avoidance: Short advisories range from "Please don't invest in Chinese stocks" to stronger language. "Don't buy Chinese stocks, lots of inside trading and issues"
Some Investors Still Take Measured Exposure: A number hold major names (Tencent, Alibaba, PDD) but often as a modest part of a diversified portfolio. "I own PDD, TCEHY, BABA and BIDU. Reasonable amount of exposure but not going mad as a %..."

62265089
>>62265087
>How People Actually Invest (Practical Options)
Via Hong Kong Listings / H-Shares / ADRs: "It is easy for foreigners to invest in H shares (HK listed shares of Chinese companies)."
Onshore For Residents With Permits: With work/residence permits you can ask banks or platforms like ExpatInvest to set up access. "If you have a Work Permit and Residence Permit... ask your bank directly or use the platform ExpatInvest."
Local Apps & Funds: Use Chinese bank apps or Alipay to buy funds and debt instruments if you live there. "my chinese banking app (cmb) has investment options built in... Alipay, you can buy fund and debt"
ETF / Filtered Approaches: Consider China-focused ETFs with quality screens or EM funds excluding China if concerned. "I now have some China exposure... DFAX was where I landed instead of VXUS."

62265093
>>62265085
I personally have some chinsese stocks I'd like to invest in, but reading all this I am fearful taht the companies I invest in the long run will just rug my money.

-I want to get in rockets, aviation, naval and wind turbines.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265100
>>62265085
I invest in rice eating bacteria manufacturing companies.

62265116
Whats with these faggot new bots that use green text in lieu of reddit bullet points...

62265120
>>62265116
That's all the greentext they can afford after today's dip.

item.title: PACK. A. SANDWHICH. WITH. SOME. CHIPS. — 132 replies — 229.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
PACK. A. SANDWHICH. WITH. SOME. CHIPS.

Open thread • Replies: 132 • Updated: 2026-05-23 07:03 UTC

OP

PACK. A. SANDWHICH.

WITH. SOME. CHIPS.

Early replies (first 3)

62262487
>>62262449
maybe don't buy guac if you are poor and it costs $4.90

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62263897
>>62263811
how much did you spend on goyslop before having this sudden flash of genius?
This is what i would be embarassed about. Also embarassing, the fact you gueninely think cooking meals is a sign of high intelligence. If you set the bar very low, that can be i guess.

62263900
>>62263602
Anon it’s time to wake up. America is like a carnival. Where the carnies are politicians and corporations looking to trick you into fake games that take all your money. It’s a predatory nation. It’s not even a nation. It’s a couple corporations. Your only hope is to work either on your own very hard, or hopefully pool resources with family. Basically look at how immigrants live. Multiple families in a house. You have to act like you lost everything and that everything is rigged to abuse you. Because it is.

62263931
>>62262449
Splurging on chips defeats the purpose. Just eat baked dark meat chicken with plain rice.

62264055
>>62263573
I also own a house and eat beef whenever I want but I'm not a loser faggot like you who contributes nothing to the conversation.
You don't even know how to talk to people and give them the wrong advice. I bet you tell people they can afford a house if they drop the beef.

62264078
>>62263931
You won't afford a house just because you stop eating chips. They are lying to you.

62264089
>>62262449
Fast food is a gigantic grift now.
They're draining what they can from low impulse control / high time preference slobs while their business model still works.
Unless you're on the road, there's no excuse for fast food anymore. It doesn't taste good, it's not healthy, and it's the same price as a normal takeout joint.

That said, the restaurant industry as a whole is a status grift for women. You don't even get good service anywhere short of fine dining. The demand is for people to say they went to X and snap a few selfies/food pictures, not to actually enjoy the meal together and hang out with their group.
I can't remember the last time I actually finished a plate before the waitstaff was trying to push the bill on us.

62264122
Caleb is fat therefore his opinion/advice is discarded

62264124
>MAGA is telling you to eat like a slave so we can continue funding wars for Israel
>the same people that squealed like pigs a few years ago because eggs were pricey

62264174
>just eat poisonous fake food goyslop, goyim
>real food is reserved for us, not you

62264324
>>62263155
Yes its especially important while developing.

62264336
>>62264324
It's about humiliation. My expensive food isn't as good unless I make you eat garbage.

62264558
>>62262607
I agree with you anon. Boomers are boomers, don’t need to beat that dead horse. But routinely eating out is retarded on many levels.

62264577
>>62264124
this lol
fucking pathetic

62264580
>>62263206
Post body fatass

62264583
>>62262454
I love Dave Ramsey. Even more than loving him, I love when poor, stupid zoomers talk about how he's a retard and doesn't know anything. Then I ask them how a retard boomer like Dave becomes worth hundreds of millions of dollars while they can't afford a running car.

62264663
>>62262607
I think my dad was 19 years old when the gold standard was abandoned. Crazy what boomers got away with.

62264675
yes, you deserve to have fast food delivered to your door. yes, you deserve to have it chewed and dropped into your mouth.

62264691
>>62264583
I can afford a running car doe

62264733
>>62264583
>you being born into a dystopian hellscape economy is the same as being born a boomer with daddy's money backing him during the most prosperous time in human history

nice bait

62264944
>>62264089
> Unless you're on the road, there's no excuse for fast food anymore. It doesn't taste good, it's not healthy, and it's the same price as a normal takeout joint.
This. You can get to go meals from a real restaurant for like $2 more if you don’t feel like cooking or whatever. Fast food isn’t a value at all. It’s a necessity when you don’t have other options. Or if you’re retarded

62264946
>>62264122
Based

62264980
>>62262862
That's common here in New Zealand as well.
I've seen people turn up with;

A whole cooked chicken and nothing else.
A 12 pack of bread rolls and a tin of Tuna.
One large bag of carrots.
A tin of Baked Beans and no can opener.
A loaf of bread and a smoked chicken breast.
Half a dozen cooked snags.
Plain rice and a chicken drumstick.
Two Sea Urchins.
A cheesy loaf.
A banana bunch.

Last nights dinner is very common.

62265020
>>62262598
you can put a loaf of bread in the fridge to make it last longer, anon

62265030
>>62263602

62265033
>>62263530
they are too retarded to understand that the shiny rocks are what go into their computers to design and make their pokemon cards

62265037
>>62265020
depends on conditions, more important for bread is air tightness and moister.. putting it in some fridges can make it go faster depending.. you can freeze bread and defrost it as you need pretty fast though

62265043
>>62265037
For toast putting the bread in the freezer is just sensible.

62265138
>>62265020
Just stick it in a bread bin like a normal person

62265148
>>62262449
boomer here

I was 30 years old when I could afford to eat at a restaurant for anything other than a special occasion. I was 40 when I could afford to eat at a restaurant whenever I wanted.

younger generations don't get a lot of pity for demanding luxuries we worked our asses off for and they think are just rights every person deserves.

same with houses. And new cars. Earn it. It took us decades of work.

62265153
>>62265138
Look at this fancy fuck with his bread humidor.

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 216 replies — 96.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 216 • Updated: 2026-05-23 05:24 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62263797
>>62259980
This.
No way they kept their expenses that low, while owning a house (so presumably in the 'burbs) throughout their 20's and had a life at the same time.
They're not going to have the social skills or personality to attract a decent girl and the wealth disparity will absolutely attract women planning to run off with half of it after five years.
>>62262729
The point of marriage is to share a life, grow together, and have a family with someone. Not to basically be bf/gf at the ripe age of 40, except with legal liabilities, while pretending there's a real bond.

62263812
>>62262791
This. If you meet a girl in your early 20's, marry by mid/late 20's, that's different. That's authentic.
This new paradigm of "not supposed to marry until you have half a mil net worth at 35+" is not what marriage was for nearly all of human history. The relationships are way less likely to be as strong and the social and financial liabilities it creates for men are nowhere close to the reward.

62263815
what's so great about kids? The amount of propaganda lately is crazy.

62263849
>>62263797
I’ll never understand the whole dink thing. just pointless to get married if you don’t plan on having kids.

62263988
>>62263605
>ask for specifics
>get vague references
>screenshot from fucking Australia
Kill yourself nigger
>Or any other million of scenarios where the pre-nup gets nullified in court.
Then show us two of them

62263992
>>62263988
you can't write a contract that violates the law.

so any time a prenup disagrees with divorce law, the prenup gets thrown out. It happens all the time.

62264003
>>62263428
>$10k or $100k
These are not the same.
But I agree with the sentiment. There's points in life where you gotta wake up and get your shit together.
>>62263605
Common Law Marriage was the most insane fucking thing when I first heard of it. In some states, if you've been dating a girl for 10 years, you legally end up considered married and have to pay shit like alimony if you break up.
It's the sort of thing that sounds like it was thought up in the 1800's, but nobody's repealed it.

62264007
>>62263653
The third person in the threesome is also another guy.

62264012
>>62263988
Not my fault you’re a retard. Why would I waste my time spoonfeeding a retard? You’re an incel anyways. Why do you care about prenups? You’ve never had sex.

62264027
>>62264003
>Common Law Marriage was the most insane fucking thing when I first heard of it. In some states, if you've been dating a girl for 10 years, you legally end up considered married and have to pay shit like alimony if you break up.
Yep, people don’t know how fucked up family law is until it starts dickslapping them in the face. What’s even funnier is when you find out that your 12 year old son actually isn’t biologically yours, but, for whatever myriad of reasons, the court doesn’t care and you gotta pay child support anyways. For a lot of states in a lot of circumstances, it actually doesn’t matter if “your” son came out of your balls or not. Your wife fucked the mailman and got caught? Sorry buddy, but that boy is still legally yours cuz reasons.

62264028
>>62259935
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing.

Why not?
>

62264045
>>62263557
Why would he be a deadbeat? If he leaves, he can send them like 300 dollars a month and they will be okay. Also pay for their college, etc

62264297
>>62263178
why do people just larp and completely invent stories on this site? is it some kind of attention seeking thing from an unresolved toddler need?

truly disgusting lot on this board. i think i won't be coming back.

62264345
>>62259935
sorry, your thread has been noticed

62264378
>>62259935
This is not the person I would mock, he actually did fucking make it. I have no idea how you get 12 years in work make near 2 million and also a fully paid house jesus christ wow.

You should mock me, I was 39, decided I wasn't wasting my 40s in a deadend job but I had 400K saved up and just said fuck it tried to invest in dividend stocks and live in my parents house they kicked me out so I tried to day trade with 200K and seven years later I am finally broke and need a job but I got a super shitty resume and no recent job history or references who remember who I am.

I am near 50 and am pretty fucked. Trying to not kill myself. Might do a bootcamp for tech with last 10K or go try a masters or do the grind Walmart -- Office -- go back to banking.

62264379
>>62264045
the most important thing about a dad is spending time with the kids, not sending 300 a month. The girl will be a whore and son turn trans without that presence. Look at Elon's kid.

62264434
>>62264379
> the most important thing about a dad is spending time with the kids
Not true. Grew up with really rich kids. Money is more important. Only brokie fathers think time with kids is more important.

62264547
>>62263992
Yeah. But you absolutely CAN structure a legal one that lets you split premarital assets. That’s been my whole point. You’re comparing apples and railroad ties.

62264551
>>62264012
>still no proof
Keep going. This is fun.
>never had sex
Woman argument. You’ve lost and it’s clear.

62264565
>>62264379
The kid will have their mom's parents, grandparents, aunt and uncles, etc. That stuff can happen regardless, even in the West with two loving parents. And he can stay in the country and visit them from time to time if he really wants.

62264595
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing.
Kek, why not? I'm on the same exact path. Life is extremely cozy. Fuck this faggot society

62264600
>>62259968
>Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.
what kind of faggot do you have to be to unironically write this shit? If you even consider getting married in le current year, you are a wheelchair bound, drool tier fucking retard.

62264605
>>62260997
based

62264672
>>62264595
You’re supposed to slave away like everyone else.
lol.
“Society” is like 95% worker bees with no choice but to toll.

62264833
>>62259935
I dislike people cooking for me. I don't know how I will be able to voice this to my future wife

62264838
>Aw geez guys I have 76 years of spending saved up at 35 years old do I have enough to retire?
Uhh, yes. Congrats. Although he'd be better off putting most of it in a trust with a reasonable $40k/yr drawdown and pursue marriage as a trustfund kid, and not have to worry about divorce after that.

62264839
>>62260039
>>62262037
These comics were highly respected in the olden days. Remember this one?

62264927
>>62259968
Marry a rich bride, retard. Fucking low class roastoid marrying nigger, just kill yourself loser.

62264930
>>62259980
Where do all you currycels come from? Unbelievable.

62265011
>>62260025
Surrogacy. Be a single dad.

item.title: NTDOY — 62 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NTDOY

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-05-23 06:05 UTC

OP

Currently Nintendo stock is extremely mispriced. All the short-term doom and gloom is priced in, ignoring the company's tremendous long-term upside. While it's not some 10x your money in a week meme coin, at this point it's a sleeper stock to hold you should know about.

Early replies (first 3)

62259388
>>62259371
its not mispriced. they make shitty games for 80+$. no one wants to buy their crap because its shit.

62259405
>NTDOY baggie
KWAB

62259407
>>62259371
not misspriced
no point in shilling your bags here btw
>>62259388
this and console era is coming to an end. Future will be only game stations with steam like platforms. retards still go for the ps5 by some sort of pavlovian reflex. it's just a standardized computer with a standardized controller. Nintendo shined for a while 30 years ago now it has become so cringe i can't stand their adds like wtf is even this shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62260032
>>62259371
>making this thread again
Please god nuke the third world

62260035
>>62259371
I agree. Not because of vidya, but because of Pokémon cards. The market cap of all PSA10 graded Pokémon cards is over $75B. Nintendo's market cap os only $53B. It makes no sense that the company that makes the cards is worth so much less than the cards themselves.

62260053
>>62260004
Nintendo could actually get away with it (releasing a Switch 3, 4, 5, etc. forever) by not being so retarded and underwhelming… Instead they will be forced to invent something else that may or may not be successful.

62260188
>>62259371
Thanks

62260304
>>62259371
agreed p/e is slurpable, nintendo aint going away

62260335
>>62259884
already priced in

62260390
Apple is apple

62260407
I bought a Rog ALLY X and it’s 100000x better than the switch I had for my son. You know the target audience? My kid uses the Ally to watch YouTube play actual fun games. He cares more about Spider-Man than bing bing wahoo slop. Nintendo failed to capture the audience by marketing to childless manchildren just like Disney Adults. They have no market share and sell an inferior product. Pokémon still looks and plays like an indie game from 2005. (lol)

Enjoy your bags tendie you get what you deserve.

62260606
>>62260407

62261314
Sorry about your hopes of pumping your bags OP, but I'll just leave this here...
>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=luiKV1nHB1w

62261355
>>62259371
The switch 2 is overpriced. People who have money don't even know what it is. It also demotes the value of the switch one, which partially went subscription based model. So it was bad business design.

62261416
Tell me the last time they ever release a ground breaking software or hardware? The new pokemon is not a cashgrab.

62261518
anyway, you know when a retard here shill some stock:
1/ he is clueless no amount of shiling here will pump it not even 0.01%

2/therefore it's a bad pick

it's cringe.last retard shilled mc donald like it was some secret gem. The real blackpill is whatever gets shilled here don't buy it or even better sell it if you have some (pokemon cards, random crypto that miraculously pumped x10, nintengoy stocks, mc goynald stock, whatever)

62261624
>>62260035
>It makes no sense that the company that makes the cards is worth so much less than the cards themselves.
What psychogenic kush are you smoking? It makes a ton of sense. By your logic, a goldsmith can't have created a masterpiece work that he has sold 20 years ago but which is now recognized to be more valuably than his entire shop.

62261625
Value Trap 2: Electric Boogaloo

62261640
>>62259371
I agree. They are the one last big truly JP dev remaining, huge franchise value that's only getting better, the only one console with exclusives, ninety-something % of the serious handheld business with proper customer attachment, JP approach to quality as opposed to deadlines, no DEI, success with theme park now expanding, huge success with the films now.
No reason not to own their stock, I think it's a good 50% undervalued.

62261642
>2009
>Nintendo has suffered a drawdown of 50%
>ZOMG invest in Nintendo!!
>keeps dropping
>10 years to reach the position you entered

Yeah nah, I know a correction when I see it.

62261677
>>62261625
kek.
What is your average OP?
Buying this above 2 is like giving to a charity

62262033
>>62261314
Already priced in

62262739
6months performance : - 45%
1 month perfomance : -12%
today's perfomance : - 0.47%

wow troontendgoy really IS daw new millionaire maker!

62263011
>>62259371
Kids don't buy games anymore. If they play games, it's free shit like fortnite and roblox

62263036
>>62263011
Also they know how to pirate.

62263054
>>62261640
>DEI
Holy shit lmaoo, it's so cringe in vidya DEI is still a hot topic while all other sectors of the economy have moved on 2 years ago. Drenched with pozz.

62263652
>>62262739
>anon argues against buying dips
I reckon you're one of those faggots who buys high and sells low

62263715
>>62259371
People aren't interested in bing bing wahoo anymore.

62263737
Nintendo even fucked over collectors with the Switch 2, I just can't justify buying physical games when they're not even on cart, this shit will have zero resale value in 10 years when the servers are no longer around. And buying digital is retarded unless they're extremely discounted.

62264904
>>62259407
>console era is coming to an end
xbox is dead and playstation is reversing its multiplat strategy so no

62264915
>>62259371
It’s not, Nintendo is failing hard lately. It is a slop company of shit now. Switch 2 was a massive disappointment. No innovation or anything just slightly better specs. Nintendo has ran out of ideas and are in the milking phase of their company, and the cows utters are almost dry

62264949
>>62263652
buy the dip is for spy, anon

62265067
>>62264904
Reversing to sell PS6, then unreversing. Nah, not gonna fall for it.

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 124 replies — 24.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 124 • Updated: 2026-05-23 02:38 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62241339
>>62219942
Soon I will get to that 1 BTC mark, RN I'm buying more and staking some.

62244007
>>62238430
That's the right move anon

62244072
>>62219942
Bitcoin is getting fucked by the Jews

62246429
>>62241339
I know you can do it anon

62246430
>>62239105
If it has a high likelihood to make me rich then I dont care

62246437
>>62244072
it's just the cycle

62246447
>>62219942
I don't want to buy btc

62246571
>>62221733
>Its ok. I have monkey NFTs. They are the future
It still blows my mind that people thought rare Pepes could actually be a real thing.

62248268
>>62244072
Including you?

62248275
>>62222594
Bad actors are not going to have access to a quantum computer before things get figured out.

62249623
>>62246447
No problem lol

62251167
>>62246571
I think he's just being sarcastic lol

62252826
>>62219942
based
>t. living in (((hyperinflation)))
read about monero too
its the final solution to the taxman question and makes keynesian jews seethe

62252834
>>62219942
I mined 24 bitcoin on a RX480 in 2011 and I still have them.

62252845
>>62252834
truly based if true

62252856
someone from /pol had unironically sent me some btc in 2020 when kikes blew up our port
and bankrupt our central banks
if I didn't need the cash and had to sell for fiat I would have a much needed two thousand fucking dollars right now
keep stacking brothers
death to fiat

62253066
>>62252834
RX480 was released in 2016 you LARPer

62253216
>>62252856
Crazy I'm from lebanon too and someone didn't send me any btc because I'm not a serial begger, I too could use a very much needed 2k right now crazy!

62253224
>>62252856
This guys an israeli scammer btw he just makes scams out of any trend clearly he struck it big in 2020 as he said someone sent him multiple bitcoins and he's still here pretending.
He know he isn't from Lebanon but his location came up Florida multiple times when the sharty hack was released he was a trial janitor here.

62253228
>>62252856
He's also pretending his sister killed herself and asking for money for it in XMR threads.
Last it was his dad that died but only after he got 8k for it.
Fucker has probably got 7 figures

62254644
>>62253228
8K?
nigger I would fucking beat you up just because you're that spiteful
I never claimed I had a sister
>>62253216
iza 3anjad e7ki 3arabe yalla
>>62253224
>He know he isn't from Lebanon but his location came up Florida multiple times when the sharty hack was released he was a trial janitor here.
kek
I posted proof so many times
seethe more jew

62254676
>>62253224
>multiple bitcoins
>multiple bitcoins
>multiple bitcoins
how dense are you
I even showed the screencap it was like 300$ total

62255610
>>62254676
>>62254644
Ah look the scammers still here even after mass reporting my thread lol, schizo scammer melty schizo melty lol fucking faggot I got your number, next your identity.
I got some faggots from /g/ working on it.
You just thought you were immune to exposure.
You're dead wrong and this time I want to see who you are.

62256655
>>62255610
Moron

62258565
>>62253066
For real?

62261255
>>62254644
Stay away Lebanon boy

62261309
>>62219942
Why would i buy a logarithmic asset when there are exponential...shit even linear

62261638
>>62261309

62261762
Get at least 10 XMR as well

62264801
>>62248275
